React Beginner to Advanced Series — Part 4C (Final Part)
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Non-member ? click here to read.
If you’ve made it this far, congratulations!
You’ve learned what React Components are, why they exist, how to create them, how they work together through nesting and composition, and how professional applications organize them.
Now it’s time to learn what separates a beginner React developer from a professional one.
Knowing how to create components is important.
Knowing how to design good components is what makes your applications scalable, maintainable, and enjoyable to work on.
Let’s explore the best practices followed by experienced React developers and avoid the mistakes that almost every beginner makes.
Common Mistakes Every Beginner Makes
Every React developer has made these mistakes at some point. The goal isn’t to avoid making mistakes altogether — it’s to recognize them early and build better habits.
1. Creating One Giant Component
A common temptation is to keep adding code to a single component because “it’s working.”
